question: Based on the median income map, which statement best describes the relationship between states?

options.A: Tennessee and Nevada have similar median incomes.
options.B: The median income differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.C: All states have very diverse median income levels.
options.D: The median income gap between Tennessee and Nevada might exceeds that between New Hampshire and Connecticut.

explanation: Tennessee (51.2$k) and Nevada (55.1$k) have a 3.9$k median income difference, while New Hampshire (29.7$k) and Connecticut (30.2$k) have only a 0.5$k difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Tennessee and Nevada into the same color bin (50-60$k range), making their large difference appear similar, while New Hampshire and Connecticut appear more different due to different color bins.
